questions.text: Pi bonds are formed by overlapping of :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: π-bonds are formed by parallel overlapping of non-hybrid orbitals ,hence this is the correct option.
Head to head overlap of hybrid orbitals forms sigma bonds ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Only non-hybrid orbitals can form pi bonds ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Hybrid orbitals can not form pi bonds ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: π-bonds are formed by parallel overlapping of un-hybrid orbitals.

questions.text: Cl2 molecule is formed by the overlap of :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The Cl2 molecule is formed by the overlap of two chlorine atoms' atomic orbitals. Specifically, it is formed by the head-to-head overlap of two chlorine 3p orbitals, resulting in a sigma (σ) bond ,hence this is an incorrect option.

options.text: p-p head on overlapping of orbitals
options.explanation: The Cl2 molecule is formed by the overlap of two chlorine atoms' atomic orbitals. Specifically, it is formed by the head-to-head overlap of two chlorine 3p orbitals, resulting in a sigma (σ) bond ,hence this is the correct option.
Both atoms are Cl, therefore, bond will be formed between the same orbitals(3p orbital) ,hence this option is incorrect.
In parallel overlapping, pi bonds are formed and in Cl2 (chlorine gas), there is only one type of covalent bond, which is a sigma (σ) bond ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: The Cl2 molecule is formed by the overlap of two chlorine atoms' atomic orbitals. Specifically, it is formed by the head-to-head overlap of two chlorine 3p orbitals, resulting in a sigma (σ) bond ,hence this is the correct option.

questions.text: In which of following species central atom is not sp3 hybridized ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In the case of NH4+ (ammonium ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp3. It forms four sigma bonds with four hydrogen atoms, and the central nitrogen atom undergoes hybridization to accommodate these bonds. This results in the formation of four equivalent sigma bonds and a tetrahedral molecular geometry around the nitrogen atom.

The NH2- ion has a bent (angular) shape with bond angles of approximately 104.5 degrees due to repulsion between lone pairs and bonding pairs. It has sp3 hybridization and two lone pairs ,hence this is an incorrect option

In NH3 (ammonia), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp3. The nitrogen atom forms three sigma bonds with three hydrogen atoms, and there is one lone pair of electrons on nitrogen. This results in a tetrahedral molecular geometry around the nitrogen atom, which corresponds to sp3 hybridization.

In NO3- (Nitrate ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp2. The nitrogen atom forms one sigma bond with one of the oxygen atoms and two pi bonds with the other two oxygen atoms, resulting in a total of three bonds. This corresponds to sp2 hybridization, and the nitrogen atom has one lone pair of electrons. The molecule has a trigonal planar molecular geometry around the central nitrogen atom ,hence this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: In NO3- (Nitrate ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp2. The nitrogen atom forms one sigma bond with one of the oxygen atoms and two pi bonds with the other two oxygen atoms, resulting in a total of three bonds. This corresponds to sp2 hybridization, and the nitrogen atom has one lone pair of electrons. The molecule has a trigonal planar molecular geometry around the central nitrogen atom.

questions.text: Which one of the followings can form strong sigma bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While sp3 hybridized orbitals can form sigma bonds, these bonds are generally weaker compared to sp-sp hybridized sigma bonds due to less effective overlap. The sp3 hybrid orbitals are arranged in a tetrahedral geometry, which results in longer bond lengths and less bond strength.

This type of hybridization involves two atoms, each with an sp hybridized orbital, forming a sigma bond. In sp hybridization, one s orbital and one p orbital mix to create two sp hybrid orbitals, which are linearly arranged, allowing for maximum overlap and thus forming a strong sigma bond. An example is the sigma bond between two carbon atoms in acetylene (C2H2).

In this hybridization, an atom with an sp3 hybridized orbital forms a sigma bond with an atom that has an sp2 hybridized orbital. Although these bonds can be strong, they do not match the strength of sp-sp sigma bonds due to less optimal orbital overlap and longer bond lengths.

This statement is incorrect as the strength of sigma bonds varies based on the hybridization of the orbitals involved. sp-sp sigma bonds are typically stronger due to better orbital overlap and shorter bond lengths compared to sp3-sp3 or sp3-sp2 bonds.

questions.explanation: This type of hybridization involves two atoms, each with an sp hybridized orbital, forming a sigma bond. In sp hybridization, one s orbital and one p orbital mix to form two sp hybrid orbitals. These orbitals are linearly arranged. An example of sp-sp hybridization can be observed in the formation of a sigma bond between two carbon atoms in acetylene (C2H2).

questions.text: End to end overlapping of orbitals give rise to the formation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A sigma (σ) bond is formed by the end-to-end or head-on overlap of atomic orbitals, creating a strong bond along the axis connecting the two nuclei. This is the correct answer.
A pi (π) bond results from the side-to-side overlap of p orbitals. This overlap occurs above and below the axis of the bonded atoms and is not an end-to-end overlap, so this is incorrect.
Metallic bonds involve a 'sea of electrons' that are shared among a lattice of metal atoms, rather than specific orbital overlap. Therefore, this does not apply to end-to-end overlapping.
A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond, is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. It does not specifically involve end-to-end overlapping of orbitals.

questions.explanation: End to end overlapping of orbitals is axial overlapping and is linear which results in sigma bond.

questions.text: Bonding in MgO is an example of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The bonding in MgO is an example of ionic bonding. This is because the difference in electronegativity between magnesium and oxygen is large (3.22). This means that magnesium is more likely to lose electrons and oxygen is more likely to gain electrons. When these two atoms bond, magnesium loses two electrons and oxygen gains two electrons, forming Mg2+ and O2- ions. These ions are then attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, forming an ionic bond.
The bonding in MgO is an example of ionic bonding. This is because the difference in electronegativity between magnesium and oxygen is large (3.22).
The bonding in MgO is an example of ionic bonding. This is because the difference in electronegativity between magnesium and oxygen is large (3.22). It is not a covalent bond.
The bonding in MgO is an example of ionic bonding. This is because the difference in electronegativity between magnesium and oxygen is large (3.22). It is not a coordinate covalent bond.

questions.explanation: The bonding in MgO is an example of ionic bonding. This is because the difference in electronegativity between magnesium and oxygen is large (3.22). This means that magnesium is more likely to lose electrons and oxygen is more likely to gain electrons. When these two atoms bond, magnesium loses two electrons and oxygen gains two electrons, forming Mg2+ and O2- ions. These ions are then attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, forming an ionic bond.

questions.text: The process in which the orbitals of different energies and shape mix with each other to give equivalent hybrid orbitals is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Dissolution involves a solute dissolving into a solvent, forming a solution. It does not involve atomic orbitals, hence it is incorrect.
Resonance refers to electron delocalization within molecules, represented by multiple structures. It does not involve mixing orbitals, making it incorrect.
Hybridization is the process where atomic orbitals with different energies and shapes mix to produce new, equivalent hybrid orbitals. This is the correct choice.
Ionization involves adding or removing electrons to form ions, unrelated to orbital mixing, thus it is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer, Hybridization, refers to the mixing of atomic orbitals with different energies and shapes to form new, equivalent hybrid orbitals. This process is crucial in explaining the geometry of molecular bonding. The other options are incorrect because: Dissolution pertains to the dissolving of substances, not orbital mixing.Resonance involves the representation of molecules by multiple structures, not the formation of hybrid orbitals.Ionization is about electron transfer to form ions, not creating hybrid orbitals.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3 is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

questions.text: _ also known as a dative bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. In an ionic bond, the metal donates one or more electrons to the nonmetal, which results in the formation of oppositely charged ions. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

No, covalent bond is not also known as a dative bond. A covalent bond is a type of chemical bond where two atoms share a pair of electrons. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. A dative bond is also a type of covalent bond, but it is a special type of covalent bond where one atom donates both electrons to the other.

A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

No, metallic bond and dative bond are two different types of bonds.Metallic bond is a type of chemical bond that holds together metal atoms in a solid. It is formed by the delocalized electrons of the metal atoms. The delocalized electrons are free to move throughout the metal, which gives metals their characteristic properties, such as conductivity, malleability, and ductility.Dative bond (also called coordinate bond) is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. It is formed when an atom with a lone pair of electrons donates that pair to an atom that is electron deficient.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: All of the following species have dative bond except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: OH-(hydroxide ion) does not have a dative bond. It is an example of a negatively charged ion formed through ionic bonding.Therefore ,this is the correct option.
Boron atom has empty 2p orbital after formation of BF3, so it can accept lone pair of fluorine atom (F atom has 3 lone pairs) and form BF4 ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Nitrogen has one lone pair left after forming 3 covalent bonds with hydrogen, it forms coordinate bond by sharing that lone pair with H ion ,hence this is an incorrect option.
In case of H3O+ coordinate/dative bond is formed between lone pair of Oxygen atom and empty s orbital of H ion ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: OH-(hydroxide ion) does not have a dative bond. It is an example of a negatively charged ion formed through ionic bonding.

questions.text: The percentage of co-ordinate covalent bond present in BF4- and NH4+ respectively :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: BF4- and NH4+ both have 3 covalent bonds and 1 co-ordinate covalent bond in their structure. Hence out of total 4 bonds 1 is co-ordinate covalent and each bond has 25% co-ordinate covalent character. (%age of dative bond = 1×100/4 = 25%)

BF4- and NH4+both have 3 covalent bonds and 1 co-ordinate covalent bond in their structure. Hence out of total 4 bonds 1 is co-ordinate covalent and each bond has 25% co-ordinate covalent character. (%age of dative bond = 1×100/4 = 25%)

BF4- and NH4+ both have 3 covalent bonds and 1 co-ordinate covalent bond in their structure. Hence out of total 4 bonds 1 is co-ordinate covalent and cach bond has 25% co-ordinate covalent character. (%age of dative bond = 1×100/4 = 25%)

BF4- and NH4+ both have 3 covalent bonds and 1 co-ordinate covalent bond in their structure. Hence out of total 4 bonds 1 is co-ordinate covalent and cach bond have 25% co-ordinate covalent character. (%age of dative bond = 1×100/4 = 25%)

questions.explanation: BF4- and NH4+ both have 3 covalent bonds and 1 co-ordinate covalent bond in their structure. Hence out of total 4 bonds 1 is co-ordinate covalent and cach bond have 25% co-ordinate covalent character. (%age of dative bond = 1×100/4 = 25%)

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is an atom or molecule that can accept electrons. In the context of NH3 and BF3, boron in BF3 acts as the acceptor because it has an empty orbital to accept electrons. NH3, however, does not act as the acceptor in this bond.

A donor species is a molecule or ion that donates a pair of electrons to form a bond. In the bond between NH3 and BF3, NH3 acts as the donor because the nitrogen atom donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3, forming a coordinate covalent bond.
A neutral species is one that has no net charge. While NH3 is neutral overall, this option does not address the role of NH3 as a donor in the formation of a coordinate covalent bond with BF3.
A negatively charged species is called an anion, formed when an atom gains electrons. NH3 is not negatively charged; instead, it donates a lone pair of electrons, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B

questions.text: Co-ordinate covalent bond is present in :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The nitride ion (N⁻3) does not have a dative bond.Dative bonds involve the sharing of a pair of electrons, with both electrons coming from one atom. Nitride ions do not exhibit this type of bonding.

Dative bonds typically involve the sharing of a pair of electrons, with both electrons contributed by one atom or ion. In the case of the chloronium ion, the bond formation is mainly due to electron transfer rather than the sharing of electron pairs, so it doesn't involve a dative bond.

Yes, the hydronium ion (H3O+) contains a dative bond. The hydronium ion is formed when a water molecule (H2O) donates a lone pair of electrons to a hydrogen ion (H+), resulting in the formation of H3O+. In this process, one oxygen atom in water donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen ion, creating a coordinate covalent or dative bond ,hence this the correct answer.
This option is incorrect in the light of explanations given above.

questions.explanation: Yes, the hydronium ion (H3O+) contains a dative bond. The hydronium ion is formed when a water molecule (H2O) donates a lone pair of electrons to a hydrogen ion (H+), resulting in the formation of H3O+. In this process, one oxygen atom in water donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen ion, creating a coordinate covalent or dative bond ,hence this the correct answer.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has the maximum number of dative bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The molecule that has the maximum number of dative bonds is B3N3H6.A dative bond is a type of chemical bond that is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. In B3N3H6, each of the boron atoms donates a pair of electrons to one of the nitrogen atoms. This gives each nitrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

H2O2 has two dative bonds. The oxygen atom on the left side of the molecule donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the right side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons. The oxygen atom on the right side of the molecule also donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the left side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.
NH4- has one dative bond. The nitrogen atom donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

In Al2O6, each aluminum atom is bonded to three oxygen atoms. The aluminum atom has a valence of 3, and each oxygen atom has a valence of 2. To satisfy the octet rule, each aluminum atom shares one pair of electrons with each oxygen atom. This results in two dative bonds in each aluminum atom. However, there are no other dative bonds in the molecule. The oxygen atoms do not have any lone pairs of electrons that they can donate to the aluminum atoms.

questions.explanation: Option Ais correct.The molecule that has the maximum number of dative bonds is B3N3H6.A dative bond is a type of chemical bond that is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. In B3N3H6, each of the boron atoms donates a pair of electrons to one of the nitrogen atoms. This gives each nitrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

questions.text: The compound which contains both ionic and covalent bond:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH4 has covalent bonding only. The carbon atom forms covalent bonds with four hydrogen atoms by sharing electrons, resulting in a stable tetrahedral structure. There are no ionic bonds in this compound.

KCN contains both ionic and covalent bonds. The bond between potassium (K) and the CN group is ionic, as potassium donates an electron. Within the CN group, the carbon and nitrogen atoms share electrons, forming a covalent bond.
H2 consists only of a covalent bond where two hydrogen atoms share their valence electrons equally, forming a single covalent bond. There are no ionic bonds present.
KCl is characterized by ionic bonding alone. Potassium transfers an electron to chlorine, forming K+ and Cl- ions, which are held together by electrostatic attraction. No covalent bonds are present.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.The compound that contains both ionic and covalent bonds is KCN. In KCN, the potassium (K) atom is a metal and the nitrogen (N) atom is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference between potassium and nitrogen is 2.1, which is considered to be a large difference. This means that the bond between potassium and nitrogen is mostly ionic. However, the nitrogen atom in KCN also has a lone pair of electrons. This lone pair of electrons can be donated to the potassium atom to form a coordinate covalent bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons.

questions.text: BF3 is an electron pair acceptor and complete its octet by accepting:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect. Accepting a single electron would not complete boron's octet.
Correct. BF3 accepts a pair of electrons to complete boron's octet through a coordinate covalent bond.
Incorrect. Accepting three electrons would provide a total of seven electrons, which does not complete the octet.
Incorrect. Accepting four electrons would exceed the octet rule for boron in BF3.

questions.explanation: BF3 (boron trifluoride) is an electron pair acceptor because boron has an incomplete octet in its valence shell. It readily accepts a pair of electrons from other atoms, forming coordinate covalent bonds, to complete its octet and achieve a stable electron configuration. By accepting an electron pair from three fluorine atoms, boron achieves a full valence shell and forms three covalent bonds, resulting in the BF3 molecule.

questions.text: Non-polar covalent bond is formed between:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Non-polar covalent bonds form between two atoms of the same element or between different elements that share the electrons equally. For example, an oxygen atom can bond with another oxygen atom to fill its outer shell.

If the two atoms in a covalent bond have different electronegativities, the electron pair is pulled closer to the more electronegative atom, resulting in a polar covalent bond. Examples of heteronuclear diatomic molecules include HCl, CO, and HF.

The term "similar atoms" is vague and doesn't provide enough information to determine whether a bond would be polar or nonpolar. If the atoms have similar electronegativities, a nonpolar covalent bond may be formed, but if they have different electronegativities, a polar covalent bond would result.
If the atoms in a covalent bond are dissimilar and have different electronegativities, the bond is likely to be polar covalent rather than nonpolar.

questions.explanation: Option A: Non-polar covalent bonds form between two atoms of the same element or between different elements that share the electrons equally. For example, an oxygen atom can bond with another oxygen atom to fill its outer shell.Option B: Heteronuclear diatomic molecules: If the two atoms in a covalent bond have different electronegativities, the electron pair is pulled closer to the more electronegative atom, resulting in a polar covalent bond. Examples of heteronuclear diatomic molecules include HCl, CO, and HF. Option C: Similar atoms: The term "similar atoms" is vague and doesn't provide enough information to determine whether a bond would be polar or nonpolar. If the atoms have similar electronegativities, a nonpolar covalent bond may be formed, but if they have different electronegativities, a polar covalent bond would result.Option D: Dissimilar atoms: If the atoms in a covalent bond are dissimilar and have different electronegativities, the bond is likely to be polar covalent rather than nonpolar.

questions.text: The bonding pair of electrons are equally shared between the atoms in?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In hydrogen fluoride (HF), the bonding pair of electrons is not equally shared between the atoms. Fluorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, so it attracts the bonding electrons closer to itself, resulting in a polar covalent bond. As a result, HF has a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atom and a partial negative charge on the fluorine atom.

Similar to HF, in hydrogen chloride (HCl), the bonding pair of electrons is not equally shared between the atoms. Chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, leading to a polar covalent bond. HCl has a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atom and a partial negative charge on the chlorine atom.

In water (H2O), the bonding pair of electrons is also not equally shared between the atoms. Oxygen is more electronegative than hydrogen, causing the oxygen atom to pull the electrons towards itself, resulting in a polar covalent bond. Water has a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atoms and a partial negative charge on the oxygen atom.

In hydrogen gas (H2), the bonding pair of electrons is equally shared between the two hydrogen atoms. Both hydrogen atoms have the same electronegativity, so they share the electrons equally, forming a nonpolar covalent bond.

questions.explanation: H2 forms a covalent bond with identical atoms, so the electrons in the bond must be shared equally.

questions.text: When an atom shares more than one electron, the bond formed is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Single covalent bond shares 1 pair of electrons between two atoms. Covalent bonding occurs when pairs of electrons are shared by atoms. Atoms will covalently bond with other atoms in order to gain more stability, which is gained by forming a full electron shell. By sharing their outer most (valence) electrons, atoms can fill up their outer electron shell and gain stability.

Double covalent bond shares 2 pairs of electrons between two atoms. Covalent bonding occurs when pairs of electrons are shared by atoms. Atoms will covalently bond with other atoms in order to gain more stability, which is gained by forming a full electron shell. By sharing their outer most (valence) electrons, atoms can fill up their outer electron shell and gain stability.

Triple covalent bond shares 3 pairs of electrons between two atoms. Covalent bonding occurs when pairs of electrons are shared by atoms. Atoms will covalently bond with other atoms in order to gain more stability, which is gained by forming a full electron shell. By sharing their outer most (valence) electrons, atoms can fill up their outer electron shell and gain stability.

Since more than one electron is mentioned in the statement. So, option D is correct. Covalent bonding occurs when pairs of electrons are shared by atoms. Atoms will covalently bond with other atoms in order to gain more stability, which is gained by forming a full electron shell. By sharing their outer most (valence) electrons, atoms can fill up their outer electron shell and gain stability.

questions.explanation: Covalent bonding occurs when pairs of electrons are shared by atoms. Atoms will covalently bond with other atoms in order to gain more stability, which is gained by forming a full electron shell. By sharing their outer most (valence) electrons, atoms can fill up their outer electron shell and gain stability.

questions.text: Non polar covalent bond is formed b/w which of the following:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This type of bond is commonly found in homonuclear diatomic molecules, which are made up of two atoms of the same element, such as H2, N2, or O2.

In heteronuclear diatomic molecules, which are made up of two different atoms, the atoms usually have different electronegativity values, resulting in a polar covalent bond rather than a non-polar covalent bond.
Similar atoms: similar atoms mean atoms of the same element, then a non-polar covalent bond can form between them.
Dissimilar atoms: dissimilar atoms mean atoms of different elements with very different electronegativity values, then a polar covalent or even an ionic bond may form.

questions.explanation: A non-polar covalent bond is formed between atoms that have similar electronegativity values, meaning they have equal or nearly equal sharing of electrons. This type of bond is commonly found in homonuclear diatomic molecules, which are made up of two atoms of the same element, such as H2, N2, or O2.Therefore, the correct answer is "Homonuclear diatomic molecules".

questions.text: In HF the covalent bond is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests the overlap of two s orbitals, which is not applicable to the HF bond. HF involves an s orbital from hydrogen and a p orbital from fluorine.

In the formation of HF, the 1s orbital of hydrogen overlaps with the 2Pz orbital of fluorine to form a covalent bond. The electron density from both atoms is concentrated along the bond axis, making this the correct description of the overlap.
This option suggests overlap between an s orbital and a Px orbital, which is not the case in HF. The correct overlap involves the Pz orbital of fluorine.
This option describes overlap between two Pz orbitals, which does not occur in HF. The bond involves an overlap between an s orbital from hydrogen and a Pz orbital from fluorine.

questions.explanation: In formation of HF, 1s orbital of H overlaps with 2Pz orbital to F to form bond. Therefore bonding is due to S-Pz overlapping.

questions.text: GN Lewis described which one of the following bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An ionic bond involves the transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of ions. G.N. Lewis primarily focused on electron pair sharing in covalent bonds rather than ionic bonds.

G.N. Lewis is well-known for his description of covalent bonds, where atoms share electron pairs to attain stability. His work led to the concept of the Lewis structure, which represents covalent bonds.
A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond, is a type of covalent bond where both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. Lewis's theory also encompasses this type of bond.

G.N. Lewis is credited with the foundational concepts that describe covalent bonds, including ordinary covalent bonds and coordinate covalent bonds. He focused on electron pair sharing, which applies to all these bond types.

questions.explanation: G.N. Lewis is renowned for his work on the nature of chemical bonding, particularly the sharing of electron pairs between atoms, which forms the basis of covalent bonds. His theories extend to both ordinary covalent bonds and coordinate covalent bonds. While ionic bonds involve electron transfer and are not the primary focus of Lewis's theories, his work laid the groundwork for understanding chemical bonding as a whole. Therefore, 'All of the above' is the correct answer, as it encompasses the types of bonds Lewis described.

questions.text: The table shown below gives the bond dissociation energies of single covalent bonds of carbon atoms with elements A, B, C, and D. Which of the following has the smallest atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to main text for explanation.
Refer to main text for explanation.
Refer to main text for explanation.
The smaller the atom the closer the orbital electrons are to the nucleus, making the forces and thus the bond energy highest. The answer to this question is hence C-D.

questions.explanation: The smaller the atom the closer the orbital electrons are to the nucleus, making the forces and thus the bond energy highest. The answer to this question is hence C-D.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules have zero Dipole Moment?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The CCl4 molecule has zero dipole moment. Despite the polar C-Cl bonds, the symmetrical tetrahedral shape causes the dipoles to cancel each other out.
CO2 is a linear molecule. The bond dipole moments in the opposing C=O bonds cancel each other, resulting in a zero dipole moment.
Cl2 consists of two identical atoms, resulting in no net dipole moment due to the absence of a charge difference.
Benzene (C6H6) has a symmetrical planar structure. The dipole moments of the C-H bonds cancel out, leading to a zero net dipole moment.
All the molecules listed have zero dipole moment due to their symmetrical geometries, which cause the dipole moments to cancel out.

questions.explanation: All the molecules listed have a zero dipole moment due to their symmetrical structures:CCl4: Although it has polar C-Cl bonds, the tetrahedral symmetry results in a net dipole moment of zero.CO2: The linear shape with two opposing C=O bonds results in the cancellation of dipole moments.Cl2: As it consists of identical atoms, no dipole moment arises.C6H6 (Benzene): The planar symmetrical structure leads to the cancellation of dipole moments of C-H bonds.Therefore, the correct answer is 'All of the above'.

questions.text: In crystal lattice of ice, each O-atom of water molecule is attached to
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In the solid state (ice), intermolecular interactions lead to a highly ordered but loose structure in which each oxygen atom is surrounded by four hydrogen atoms; two of these hydrogen atoms are covalently bonded to the oxygen atom, and the two others (at longer distances) are hydrogen bonded to the oxygen atom's unshared electron pairs.

In the solid state (ice), intermolecular interactions lead to a highly ordered but loose structure in which each oxygen atom is surrounded by four hydrogen atoms; two of these hydrogen atoms are covalently bonded to the oxygen atom, and the two others (at longer distances) are hydrogen bonded to the oxygen atom's unshared electron pairs.In the solid state (ice), intermolecular interactions lead to a highly ordered but loose structure in which each oxygen atom is surrounded by four hydrogen atoms; two of these hydrogen atoms are covalently bonded to the oxygen atom, and the two others (at longer distances) are hydrogen bonded to the oxygen atom's unshared electron pairs.

In ice molecule, each oxygen atom is tightly bound by four hydrogen atoms within a molecule and form tetrahedral.

questions.text: In comparison with oxygen gas, a strong triple bond is present between two nitrogen atoms in a molecule and therefore nitrogen gas is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect. Nitrogen gas is relatively unreactive due to its strong triple bond, which requires significant energy to break, leading to limited reactivity.

Nitrogen (N₂) has a strong triple bond (N≡N) with very high bond energy (~946 kJ/mol), making it chemically stable and less reactive under normal conditions. However, it is not completely inert like noble gases — it can react under specific conditions (e.g., lightning, high temperature, or with catalysts in the Haber process).

Nitrogen (N₂) has a strong triple bond (N≡N) with very high bond energy (~946 kJ/mol), making it chemically stable and less reactive under normal conditions. However, it is not completely inert like noble gases — it can react under specific conditions (e.g., lightning, high temperature, or with catalysts in the Haber process).
Incorrect. Nitrogen gas's reactivity falls between being highly reactive and completely inert, but it is more accurately described as relatively unreactive due to its triple bond.

questions.explanation: Nitrogen (N₂) has a strong triple bond (N≡N) with very high bond energy (~946 kJ/mol), making it chemically stable and less reactive under normal conditions. However, it is not completely inert like noble gases — it can react under specific conditions (e.g., lightning, high temperature, or with catalysts in the Haber process).

questions.text: GN Lewis described which one of the following bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An ionic bond involves the transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of ions. G.N. Lewis primarily focused on electron pair sharing in covalent bonds rather than ionic bonds.

G.N. Lewis is well-known for his description of covalent bonds, where atoms share electron pairs to attain stability. His work led to the concept of the Lewis structure, which represents covalent bonds.
A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond, is a type of covalent bond where both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. Lewis's theory also encompasses this type of bond.

G.N. Lewis is credited with the foundational concepts that describe covalent bonds, including ordinary covalent bonds and coordinate covalent bonds. He focused on electron pair sharing, which applies to all these bond types.

questions.explanation: G.N. Lewis is renowned for his work on the nature of chemical bonding, particularly the sharing of electron pairs between atoms, which forms the basis of covalent bonds. His theories extend to both ordinary covalent bonds and coordinate covalent bonds. While ionic bonds involve electron transfer and are not the primary focus of Lewis's theories, his work laid the groundwork for understanding chemical bonding as a whole. Therefore, 'All of the above' is the correct answer, as it encompasses the types of bonds Lewis described.

questions.text: In HF the covalent bond is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests the overlap of two s orbitals, which is not applicable to the HF bond. HF involves an s orbital from hydrogen and a p orbital from fluorine.

In the formation of HF, the 1s orbital of hydrogen overlaps with the 2Pz orbital of fluorine to form a covalent bond. The electron density from both atoms is concentrated along the bond axis, making this the correct description of the overlap.
This option suggests overlap between an s orbital and a Px orbital, which is not the case in HF. The correct overlap involves the Pz orbital of fluorine.
This option describes overlap between two Pz orbitals, which does not occur in HF. The bond involves an overlap between an s orbital from hydrogen and a Pz orbital from fluorine.

questions.explanation: In formation of HF, 1s orbital of H overlaps with 2Pz orbital to F to form bond. Therefore bonding is due to S-Pz overlapping.

questions.text: Which one of the following property belongs to covalent bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Covalent bonds are generally considered directional, meaning they have a specific spatial orientation between the participating atoms. This directionality arises from the overlap of atomic orbitals involved in the formation of the covalent bond. The specific arrangement of these overlapping orbitals determines the bond angle and geometry in a molecule. This option is not correct.

Yes, covalent compounds can exhibit isomerism. Isomerism refers to the existence of different chemical compounds that have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements of atoms. In covalent compounds, isomerism can manifest in various forms. This option is correct.

Covalent bonds themselves are typically rigid in the sense that they maintain a specific bond length and bond angle based on the arrangement of the participating atoms and the orbitals involved in the bond. The rigidity arises from the overlapping of atomic orbitals forming the covalent bond. This option is not correct.
Covalent bonds have slow reactions, this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Covalent compounds, especially organic covalent compounds, display various types of isomersims.

questions.text: The covalent compounds exhibit:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Covalent compounds exhibit fast reactions:This statement is not a characteristic of covalent compounds in general. The speed of reactions can vary for both covalent and other types of compounds and depends on various factors such as reactants, conditions, and catalysts.

b) Covalent compounds exhibit non-directional nature:This statement is partially correct. Covalent compounds exhibit a non-directional nature in their bonding. This means that covalent bonds are formed by sharing electron pairs between atoms, and there is no preferred orientation or alignment of the atoms involved in the bond.

c) Covalent compounds exhibit non-rigid structures:This statement is partially correct. Covalent compounds often exhibit non-rigid structures because the sharing of electron pairs allows atoms to rotate freely around the bond axis. This flexibility in bond rotation contributes to the non-rigid nature of covalent compounds.

d) Covalent compounds exhibit isomerism:This statement is correct. Covalent compounds can exhibit isomerism, which is the phenomenon where two or more compounds have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements or spatial orientations. This occurs due to the different ways atoms can be connected within a molecule.

questions.explanation: Covalent bonds are directional in nature. Therefore, they exhibit the phenomenon of isomerism.

questions.text: Covalent bonds are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Covalent bonds are directional. This means that the electrons that are shared between the two atoms are not evenly distributed, but are concentrated in a specific region of space. The direction of the covalent bond is determined by the overlapping orbitals of the two atoms.
Multidirectional bonds do not exist, as covalent bonds are by definition directional.
Bidirectional bonds are also not possible, as the electrons in a covalent bond are shared between two atoms
Non-directional bonds are possible in ionic bonds, but not in covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: Four covalent bonds are directed towards four corners of a regular tetrahedron. Covalent bonds are directional. This means that the electrons that are shared between the two atoms are not evenly distributed, but are concentrated in a specific region of space. The direction of the covalent bond is determined by the overlapping orbitals of the two atoms.

questions.text: If the sharing of an electron pair is unequal and the atoms have an electronegativity difference of 1.4 to 1.6, then the type of bond formed is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The bond is ionic if the E.N. difference is greater than 1.7; also, in an ionic bond there is complete donation of an electron from one atom, not sharing between atoms; hence, this is an incorrect option.

When the sharing of an electron pair in a covalent bond is unequal due to an electronegativity difference between the two atoms of approximately 1.4 to 1.6, this type of sharing is often referred to as "polar covalent bonding," as the bond between two atoms is polar covalent if the electronegativity difference between them is 0.5 to 1.6.
The bond between two atoms is non-polar covalent if electronegativity difference between them is zero or less than 0.5 ,hence this is an incorrect option.
In metallic bonding, there is delocalization of valence electrons in a "sea" or "pool" shared among many metal atoms, unlike in ionic or covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: The bond between two atoms is non-polar covalent if the electronegativity difference between them is zero or less than 0.5. The bond between two atoms is polar covalent if the electronegativity difference between them is 0.5 to 1.6. The bond between two atoms is equally covalent and ionic if the electronegativity difference is 1.7. The bond is ionic if the E.N. difference is greater than 1.7.

questions.text: Melting and boiling point of transition elements is higher due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Binding energy refers to the energy required to disassemble a whole system into separate parts. In the context of transition metals, this refers to the energy needed to separate the atoms in the metal. Transition metals have higher binding energy due to the presence of more unpaired electrons in their d-orbitals, which can form more bonds and thus require more energy to break. Hence, their boiling and melting points are high

Transition elements are characterized by their partially filled d orbitals, which provide them with unique properties, including higher melting and boiling points. Here's why:1. Presence of Unpaired d Electrons: Transition elements have incompletely filled d orbitals in their electron configuration. These unpaired electrons contribute to the formation of strong metallic bonds between the atoms in the crystal lattice. Metallic bonds are formed due to the delocalization of electrons within the crystal structure, leading to strong attractive forces between the metal atoms.2. Increased Effective Nuclear Charge: The unpaired d electrons experience a higher effective nuclear charge (the net positive charge experienced by an electron in a multi-electron atom) compared to the fully filled d orbitals in non-transition elements. This increased effective nuclear charge enhances the attraction between the nucleus and the valence electrons, contributing to the higher melting and boiling points.3. Stronger London Dispersion Forces: Transition elements have more extensive electron clouds due to the presence of the d electrons. As a result, the London dispersion forces, which are attractive forces between temporary dipoles induced by fluctuations in electron distribution, are stronger in transition elements. These forces also contribute to the higher melting and boiling points.4. Increased Electron Density: The presence of unpaired d electrons results in higher electron density in the crystal lattice. This increased electron density leads to stronger electron-electron repulsions, which in turn contribute to the higher melting and boiling points.

Hardness is a measure of how resistant a material is to various kinds of permanent shape changes when a compressive force is applied. While hardness does correlate with high melting and boiling points, it's not a direct cause. However, transition metals are generally harder due to the same reasons that give them higher melting and boiling points (i.e., more bonding opportunities from unpaired d-orbital electrons).

This option suggests that all the above factors contribute to the higher melting and boiling points of transition metals. While A and B are directly responsible, C (hardness) is more of a correlated property rather than a cause. So, while all these factors are associated with the properties of transition metals, only A and B are the direct causes of higher melting and boiling points.

questions.explanation: Transition elements are characterized by their partially filled d orbitals, which provide them with unique properties, including higher melting and boiling points. Here's why:1. Presence of Unpaired d Electrons: Transition elements have incompletely filled d orbitals in their electron configuration. These unpaired electrons contribute to the formation of strong metallic bonds between the atoms in the crystal lattice. Metallic bonds are formed due to the delocalization of electrons within the crystal structure, leading to strong attractive forces between the metal atoms.2. Increased Effective Nuclear Charge: The unpaired d electrons experience a higher effective nuclear charge (the net positive charge experienced by an electron in a multi-electron atom) compared to the fully filled d orbitals in non-transition elements. This increased effective nuclear charge enhances the attraction between the nucleus and the valence electrons, contributing to the higher melting and boiling points.3. Stronger London Dispersion Forces: Transition elements have more extensive electron clouds due to the presence of the d electrons. As a result, the London dispersion forces, which are attractive forces between temporary dipoles induced by fluctuations in electron distribution, are stronger in transition elements. These forces also contribute to the higher melting and boiling points.4. Increased Electron Density: The presence of unpaired d electrons results in higher electron density in the crystal lattice. This increased electron density leads to stronger electron-electron repulsions, which in turn contribute to the higher melting and boiling points.

questions.text: If the difference of electronegativity is 1.7 or more than that, the bond formed is said to be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct option. An ionic bond is characterized by the transfer of electrons from one atom to another, typically occurring when the electronegativity difference between the atoms is 1.7 or greater. This results in the formation of positively and negatively charged ions, which are held together by strong electrostatic forces.

Incorrect option. A covalent bond involves the sharing of electrons between atoms, generally occurring when the electronegativity difference is less than 1.7. This type of bond is typical between nonmetal atoms with similar electronegativity values.

Incorrect option. A metallic bond is found between metal atoms where electrons are shared in an electron 'sea', which allows them to move freely. This type of bond is not determined by electronegativity differences.

Incorrect option. The term 'chemical bond' is a general term that includes all types of bonds, such as ionic, covalent, and metallic. It does not specify the type of bond based on electronegativity differences.

questions.explanation: An ionic bond is formed when the electronegativity difference between two atoms is 1.7 or more. In an ionic bond, one atom (typically a metal) has a significantly lower electronegativity and tends to lose electrons to become a positively charged ion (cation), while the other atom (typically a nonmetal) has a higher electronegativity and tends to gain those electrons, becoming a negatively charged ion (anion). The strong electrostatic attraction between the oppositely charged ions results in the formation of ionic compounds with high melting and boiling points and typically solid at room temperature.

questions.text: Calculations regarding ionic character tells us that there is no bond when ionic character is _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: To find the ionic character (or the polarity) of a bond, we look at the electronegativity of the two atoms involved. The greater the difference, the more ionic character in the bond. For the entire compound, we can look at its polarity from the arrangement of polar bonds through the 3D structure of the molecule.

Criteria of electronegativity helps us to understand the nature of bond. So, in order to decide the % of ionic nature in a compound, it is better to note the difference of electronegativity between the bonded atoms. If the difference is 1.7 or more than that, then the bond is said to be ionic. Keeping this aspect in view, NaCl has 72% ionic character. CSF has 92% ionic character and calculations tell us that there is no bond with 100% ionic character. (PTB chapter 6: Chemical bonding).

Criteria of electronegativity helps us to understand the nature of bond. So, in order to decide the % of ionic nature in a compound, it is better to note the difference of electronegativity between the bonded atoms. If the difference is 1.7 or more than that, then the bond is said to be ionic. Keeping this aspect in view, NaCl has 72% ionic character. CSF has 92% ionic character and calculations tell us that there is no bond with 100% ionic character. (PTB chapter 6: Chemical bonding)Criteria of electronegativity helps us to understand the nature of bond. So, in order to decide the % of ionic nature in a compound, it is better to note the difference of electronegativity between the bonded atoms. If the difference is 1.7 or more than that, then the bond is said to be ionic. Keeping this aspect in view, NaCl has 72% ionic character. CSF has 92% ionic character and calculations tell us that there is no bond with 100% ionic character. (PTB chapter 6: Chemical bonding).

There is no bond 100% ionic in Chemistry. The greater the difference, the more ionic character in the bond. For the entire compound, we can look at its polarity from the arrangement of polar bonds through the 3D structure of the molecule.

CsF has 92% ionic character and calculations tell us that there is no bond with 100% ionic character.(PTB chapter 6: Chemical bonding)

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because Ionic bond is a type of chemical bond.
This option is correct Chemical bonding is the attraction between two or more atoms that allows them to be able to form a stable chemical compound.

questions.explanation: Correct option is B. Chemical bonding is the attraction between two or more atoms that allows them to be able to form a stable chemical compound. The specific nature of a chemical bond can vary, but the most commonly known are covalent and ionic bonds. With these bonds, it provides sufficient energy between atoms when one has less.

questions.text: Electrical conductivity depends upon _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electrical conductivity depends on temperature. The electrical conductivity of a metal decreases with an increase in temperature because the thermal energy increases the collision of electrons and disturbs the free flow.
Electrical conductivity is not significantly influenced by the degree of dilution, making this option incorrect.
The nature of the electrolyte does not directly affect electrical conductivity in the context of this question, making this option incorrect.
The speed of ions is not the primary factor determining electrical conductivity as compared to temperature, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Electrical conductivity depends on temperature. The electrical conductivity of a metal decreases with increase in temperature because the thermal energy increases the collision of electrons and disturbs the free flow.

questions.text: Which of the following is the worst conductor of electricity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because Hg being a metal has a metallic lattice whereby there is a sea of electrons that enable it to conduct electricity. Mercury (Hg) is not the worst conductor of electricity, but it is a poor conductor. It is classified as a metal, and as such, it does conduct electricity to some extent. However, compared to other metals like copper or silver, mercury has a relatively high electrical resistance. This means that it is less efficient at conducting electricity. Additionally, mercury is a liquid at room temperature, which further limits its practical use as a conductor. Therefore, while mercury is not the worst conductor overall, it is not commonly used or considered among the best conductors of electricity.

Pure distilled water is a poor conductor of electricity. Water itself is a good electrical insulator, but it can conduct electricity when it contains dissolved ions or impurities. These ions, such as sodium (Na+), chloride (Cl-), or other dissolved salts, increase the conductivity of water by allowing the flow of electric current. In order to conduct electricity, a substance must possess charged particles, either ions or electrons. Distilled water has neither of those as it does not dissociate into H+ or OH- ions unless it is dissolved with some other polar substance. Hence it does not conduct electricity and therefore option B is the correct answer.

No, NaCl (sodium chloride), when dissolved in water or in molten form, is actually a good conductor of electricity. When NaCl dissolves in water, it dissociates into sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-), which are responsible for conducting the electric current. The presence of these ions in solution allows for the movement of charged particles, facilitating the flow of electricity. As a result, NaCl solution (commonly known as saltwater) can conduct electricity relatively well compared to pure water or other non-ionic substances. Option C is incorrect because NaCl being an ionic compound, has free ions in the liquid state thus possessing free ions to conduct electricity.

A dilute sugar solution, in general, is a poor conductor of electricity. Sugar, or sucrose, is a non-ionic compound, which means it does not readily dissociate into charged particles (ions) when dissolved in water. As a result, a dilute sugar solution does not contain enough ions to facilitate the conduction of electric current.While water molecules can interact with sugar molecules and form weak hydrogen bonds, these interactions do not result in the formation of free-moving charged particles that can conduct electricity. Therefore, compared to ionic compounds like NaCl, a dilute sugar solution has significantly lower conductivity. It's important to note that highly concentrated sugar solutions or solutions that contain other ionic impurities can exhibit some level of electrical conductivity. However, in general, a dilute sugar solution would be considered a poor conductor of electricity. Option D could also be correct since sugar solution, although polar, does not possess any free ions so it cannot conduct electricity. However, in such questions, it is best to prefer water as the first choice.

questions.explanation: Pure distilled water is a poor conductor of electricity. Water itself is a good electrical insulator, but it can conduct electricity when it contains dissolved ions or impurities. These ions, such as sodium (Na+), chloride (Cl-), or other dissolved salts, increase the conductivity of water by allowing the flow of electric current. In order to conduct electricity, a substance must possess charged particles, either ions or electrons. Distilled water has neither of those as it does not dissociate into H+ or OH- ions unless it is dissolved with some other polar substance. Hence it does not conduct electricity and therefore option B is the correct answer.

questions.text: The bond between atoms of two elements having atomic numbers 37 and 53 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The elements with atomic numbers 37 and 53 are rubidium (Rb) and iodine (I), respectively. They would likely form an ionic bond, with rubidium donating an electron to iodine, resulting in the formation of the RbI ionic compound. Therefore this option is correct.

The elements with atomic numbers 37 and 53 are rubidium (Rb) and iodine (I), respectively. They would likely form an ionic bond, with rubidium donating an electron to iodine, resulting in the formation of the RbI ionic compound. Therefore this option is not correct.

The elements with atomic numbers 37 and 53 are rubidium (Rb) and iodine (I), respectively. They would likely form an ionic bond, with rubidium donating an electron to iodine, resulting in the formation of the RbI ionic compound. Therefore this option is not correct.

The elements with atomic numbers 37 and 53 are rubidium (Rb) and iodine (I), respectively. They would likely form an ionic bond, with rubidium donating an electron to iodine, resulting in the formation of the RbI ionic compound. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Electronic configuration of atom with atomic no. 37 is:1s22s22p63s23p64s23d104p65s1or [Kr]5s1Electronic configuration of atom with atomic no. 53 is:1s22s22p63s23p64s23d104p65s24d105p5or [Kr] 5s24d105p5The atom with atomic no. 53 needs 1 electron to complete its shell while the one with atomic no. 37 has 1 electron in its outermost shell. The latter can lose this electron to form a cation. This electron will be gained by the former to form an anion. In this way, an ionic or electrovalent bond will be formed between the two atoms.
hints: bond between atoms of two elements

questions.text: Energy is _ when oppositely charged ions are brought close to each other.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: When oppositely charged ions come together, they form an ionic bond which is energetically favorable. This process releases a significant amount of energy, known as lattice energy. For example, when sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-) combine to form sodium chloride (NaCl), energy is released, resulting in a negative change in enthalpy (ΔH).
This option is incorrect because energy is not absorbed when oppositely charged ions approach each other. Instead, energy is released during the formation of ionic bonds.
This option is incorrect as it suggests that there is no change in energy when ions are brought together. In reality, energy is released when oppositely charged ions form an ionic bond.
This option is also incorrect because it implies that energy levels do not change. However, the interaction between oppositely charged ions results in a release of energy, indicating a change.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Released. When oppositely charged ions approach each other, they exert attractive forces, leading to the formation of an ionic bond. This process is exothermic, meaning it releases energy into the surroundings. The concept of lattice energy is crucial here; it quantifies the energy released when gaseous ions combine to form a solid ionic lattice, such as NaCl.In contrast, Option B: Absorbed is incorrect because the formation of ionic bonds releases energy, not absorbs it. Option C: Remain same and Option D: Constant are both incorrect as they imply that there is no energy change when oppositely charged ions come together. The formation of ionic bonds is a dynamic process that involves significant energy release.

questions.text: Mostly ionic compounds are produced between elements of group:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Both groups consist of metals, which tend to lose electrons rather than form ionic bonds with each other. Ionic bonds generally form between metals and non-metals.
Group IB elements are metals, and forming ionic bonds requires a significant electronegativity difference, typically achieved between metals and non-metals. Group VIIB elements are also metals.

This is correct because Group IA and IIA elements are metals that tend to lose electrons, while Group VIIA elements (halogens) are non-metals that tend to gain electrons, leading to ionic bond formation due to significant electronegativity differences.
Both these groups contain metals, which cannot form ionic bonds with each other. Ionic bonds require the presence of a non-metal to accept electrons.

questions.explanation: In ionic compounds, one element donates electrons (typically a metal or an element with low electronegativity), becoming a positively charged cation, while the other element accepts those electrons (typically a non-metal or an element with high electronegativity), becoming a negatively charged anion. The resulting electrostatic attraction between the cations and anions holds the compound together in a lattice structure. So, ionic compounds are indeed commonly produced when elements from Group IA, IIA, and VIIA are involved in chemical reactions due to the significant electronegativity differences between these groups of elements.

questions.text: Maximum ionic character is exhibited by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.NaCl has a strong ionic bond ( 72% ionic character) but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference. CsF has greatest electronegativity difference of 3.19 and maximum ionic character of 92% ,hence this is the correct option.

Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.KCl has a strong ionic bond but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.

Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference.HCl has a strong ionic bond but does not exhibit ionic character as much as CsF do (due to its greater E.N difference between Cs and F) ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Strength of ionic character depends upon electronegativity difference. CsF has greatest electronegativity difference of 3.19 and maximum ionic character of 92%.

questions.text: Which one of the following pair of compounds contains one that is giant ionic and one that is simple molecule?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Al2O3 (aluminum oxide) is a giant ionic compound with a three-dimensional lattice of aluminum and oxygen ions. Al2Cl6 (aluminum chloride) is a molecular compound consisting of covalent bonds. This combination fits the criteria of one giant ionic and one simple molecular compound, making it the correct choice.
P4O10 (phosphorus pentoxide) and PCl3 (phosphorus trichloride) are both molecular compounds with covalent bonding. Neither is giant ionic, making this option incorrect.
SiO2 (silicon dioxide) forms a giant covalent structure, while SiCl4 (silicon tetrachloride) is a simple molecular compound. However, none is a giant ionic compound, so this option is incorrect.
HF (hydrogen fluoride) and PbCl4 (lead(IV) chloride) are both molecular compounds. Neither fits the requirement of having one giant ionic compound, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Al2O3 (aluminum oxide) is a giant ionic compound. It consists of a three-dimensional lattice of aluminum ions (Al+3) and oxygen ions (O-2), held together by ionic bonds. It forms a crystal lattice structure.Al2Cl6 (aluminum hexachloride) is a molecular compound and is made up of covalently bonded aluminum and chlorine atoms. It is not a giant ionic compound like Al2O3.

questions.text: All of the following statements are matched correctly for ionic and covalent bonds EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of elements with very different electronegativities, while covalent bonds are formed between atoms of elements with similar electronegativities.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Ionic bonds occur between elements with different electronegativities, leading to electron transfer, while covalent bonds involve electron sharing between elements with similar electronegativities.

options.text: Covalent compounds have low melting and boiling points, whereas ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points due to strong ionic interactions, while covalent compounds generally have lower melting and boiling points due to weaker intermolecular forces.

options.text: Ionic bonds are directional, while covalent bonds are non-directional.
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Ionic bonds are non-directional because they result from electrostatic attraction between ions that are not dependent on specific orientations. Covalent bonds, however, are directional because the shared electrons are attracted to specific atoms, forming a bond in a particular direction.

options.text: Covalent compounds are poor conductors of electricity, whereas ionic compounds are good conductors of electricity when molten or in solution.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Covalent compounds do not conduct electricity well because their electrons are not free to move. Ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or in solution because the ions are free to move and carry charge.

questions.explanation: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of different elements with very different electronegativities. The more electronegative atom will attract the electrons from the less electronegative atom, forming a positively charged ion (cation) and a negatively charged ion (anion). The attraction between the oppositely charged ions is what holds the ionic bond together. Because ionic bonds are formed by the transfer of electrons, they are not directional. The ions are attracted to each other regardless of their orientation. Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of the same element or of elements with similar electronegativities. The atoms share electrons in order to complete their valence shells. The shared electrons are attracted to the nuclei of both atoms, which is what holds the covalent bond together. So this is directional.

questions.text: Which molecule is least ionic?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NaCl is highly ionic due to the significant electronegativity difference between sodium (Na) and chlorine (Cl). Sodium is a metal with low electronegativity, while chlorine is a non-metal with high electronegativity, forming a strong ionic bond.
HF has a significant ionic character because of the large electronegativity difference between hydrogen (H) and fluorine (F), with fluorine being highly electronegative.

HCl is the least ionic among the given options. While chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, the electronegativity difference is not as large as in the other molecules, resulting in a bond with more covalent character.
CsF is highly ionic because cesium (Cs) is a metal with very low electronegativity and fluorine (F) is a highly electronegative non-metal, resulting in a strong ionic bond.

questions.explanation: Among the molecules NaCl, HF, HCl, and CsF, hcl is the least ionic. Ionic character in a molecule is determined by the electronegativity difference between the atoms involved. The larger the electronegativity difference, the more ionic the bond.In HCl, the electronegativity difference between hydrogen (H) and chlorine (Cl) is relatively small. Chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, but the difference is not as significant as in the other compounds listed.In contrast, NaCl, HF, and CsF have larger electronegativity differences, making their bonds more ionic. Sodium (Na) and cesium (Cs) are highly electropositive, and they form ionic compounds with chlorine (Cl) and fluorine (F), which are highly electronegative elements.So, HCl is the least ionic among the given molecules.
